page: Microsoft Azure FX96-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features an x86_64 Intel processor architecture with 24 dedicated vCPUs. The server is configured with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 76.33 GB per vCPU.

This instance includes 5669 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. It does not include any graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware configuration is designed for workloads requiring massive memory footprints and substantial local storage capacity without the need for hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX96-24mds_v2 represents a specialized, high-density resource allocation where the investment is heavily weighted toward memory capacity and local storage rather than raw compute core count. This makes the server highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale in-memory databases, heavy caching layers, enterprise relational databases, and complex data analytics pipelines that require massive RAM allocations per active thread.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 Intel processor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs paired with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 1832.0 GB, translating to 76.33 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance is equipped with 5669 GB of local disk space and supports Premium Storage. It does not include GPU accelerators. This server is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale in-memory databases, and data analytics workloads requiring massive memory density relative to compute cores.
